252: void Utility::translateOpaqueConfig(const ProtobufWkt::Any& typed_config,
253:                                     ProtobufMessage::ValidationVisitor& validation_visitor,
254:                                     Protobuf::Message& out_proto) {
255:   static const std::string struct_type = ProtobufWkt::Struct::default_instance().GetTypeName();
256:   static const std::string typed_struct_type =
257:       xds::type::v3::TypedStruct::default_instance().GetTypeName();
258:   static const std::string legacy_typed_struct_type =
259:       udpa::type::v1::TypedStruct::default_instance().GetTypeName();
260:   if (!typed_config.value().empty()) {
261:     // Unpack methods will only use the fully qualified type name after the last '/'.
262:     // https://github.com/protocolbuffers/protobuf/blob/3.6.x/src/google/protobuf/any.proto#L87
263:     absl::string_view type = TypeUtil::typeUrlToDescriptorFullName(typed_config.type_url());
264: 
265:     if (type == typed_struct_type) {
266:       xds::type::v3::TypedStruct typed_struct;
267:       MessageUtil::unpackToOrThrow(typed_config, typed_struct);
268:       // if out_proto is expecting Struct, return directly
269:       if (out_proto.GetTypeName() == struct_type) {
270:         out_proto.CheckTypeAndMergeFrom(typed_struct.value());
271:       } else {
272:         // The typed struct might match out_proto, or some earlier version, let
273:         // MessageUtil::jsonConvert sort this out.
274: #ifdef ENVOY_ENABLE_YAML
275:         MessageUtil::jsonConvert(typed_struct.value(), validation_visitor, out_proto);
276: #else
277:         IS_ENVOY_BUG("Attempting to use JSON typed structs with JSON compiled out");
278: #endif
279:       }
280:     } else if (type == legacy_typed_struct_type) {
281:       udpa::type::v1::TypedStruct typed_struct;
282:       MessageUtil::unpackToOrThrow(typed_config, typed_struct);
283:       // if out_proto is expecting Struct, return directly
284:       if (out_proto.GetTypeName() == struct_type) {
285:         out_proto.CheckTypeAndMergeFrom(typed_struct.value());
286:       } else {
287:         // The typed struct might match out_proto, or some earlier version, let
288:         // MessageUtil::jsonConvert sort this out.
289: #ifdef ENVOY_ENABLE_YAML
290:         MessageUtil::jsonConvert(typed_struct.value(), validation_visitor, out_proto);
291: #else
292:         UNREFERENCED_PARAMETER(validation_visitor);
293:         IS_ENVOY_BUG("Attempting to use legacy JSON structs with JSON compiled out");
294: #endif
295:       }
296:     } // out_proto is expecting Struct, unpack directly
297:     else if (type != struct_type || out_proto.GetTypeName() == struct_type) {
298:       MessageUtil::unpackToOrThrow(typed_config, out_proto);
299:     } else {
300: #ifdef ENVOY_ENABLE_YAML
301:       ProtobufWkt::Struct struct_config;
302:       MessageUtil::unpackToOrThrow(typed_config, struct_config);
303:       MessageUtil::jsonConvert(struct_config, validation_visitor, out_proto);
304: #else
305:       IS_ENVOY_BUG("Attempting to use JSON structs with JSON compiled out");
306: #endif
307:     }
308:   }
309: }
310: 
311: absl::StatusOr<JitteredExponentialBackOffStrategyPtr>
312: Utility::buildJitteredExponentialBackOffStrategy(
313:     absl::optional<const envoy::config::core::v3::BackoffStrategy> backoff,
314:     Random::RandomGenerator& random, const uint32_t default_base_interval_ms,
315:     absl::optional<const uint32_t> default_max_interval_ms) {
316:   // BackoffStrategy config is specified
317:   if (backoff != absl::nullopt) {
318:     uint32_t base_interval_ms = PROTOBUF_GET_MS_REQUIRED(backoff.value(), base_interval);
319:     uint32_t max_interval_ms =
320:         PROTOBUF_GET_MS_OR_DEFAULT(backoff.value(), max_interval, base_interval_ms * 10);
321: 
322:     if (max_interval_ms < base_interval_ms) {
323:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(
324:           "max_interval must be greater than or equal to the base_interval");
325:     }
326:     return std::make_unique<JitteredExponentialBackOffStrategy>(base_interval_ms, max_interval_ms,
327:                                                                 random);
328:   }
329: 
330:   // default_base_interval_ms must be greater than zero
331:   if (default_base_interval_ms == 0) {
332:     return absl::InvalidArgumentError("default_base_interval_ms must be greater than zero");
333:   }
334: 
335:   // default maximum interval is specified
336:   if (default_max_interval_ms != absl::nullopt) {
337:     if (default_max_interval_ms.value() < default_base_interval_ms) {
338:       return absl::InvalidArgumentError(
339:           "default_max_interval_ms must be greater than or equal to the default_base_interval_ms");
340:     }
341:     return std::make_unique<JitteredExponentialBackOffStrategy>(
342:         default_base_interval_ms, default_max_interval_ms.value(), random);
343:   }
344:   // use default base interval
345:   return std::make_unique<JitteredExponentialBackOffStrategy>(
346:       default_base_interval_ms, default_base_interval_ms * 10, random);
347: }
348: 
349: } // namespace Config
350: } // namespace Envoy

I am thinking...: Now I have a good understanding of the codebase. Let me summarize the key files that implement xDS v2 and v3 API handling in the Envoy repository:

1. **`source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.cc`** - This is the core file that maps xDS resource type URLs to versioned service methods. It explicitly mentions both v2 and v3 service names in comments (e.g., "envoy.api.v2.RouteDiscoveryService" and "envoy.service.route.v3.RouteDiscoveryService"). It builds a map from resource type URLs to v3 service methods (SotW, Delta, REST).

2. **`source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.h`** - Header for the above.

3. **`source/common/config/utility.h`** - Contains `checkTransportVersion()` which validates the transport API version (V2 vs V3) and emits deprecation warnings for V2.

4. **`source/common/config/utility.cc`** - Implementation of utility functions for xDS config handling.

5. **`source/common/config/subscription_factory_impl.cc`** - Factory that creates subscriptions based on config source type, calls `checkTransportVersion()`.

6. **`source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_mux_impl.cc`** - The legacy SotW gRPC mux implementation using `envoy::service::discovery::v3::DiscoveryRequest/Response`. The factory creates `GrpcMuxImpl` for SotW ADS.

7. **`source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/grpc_mux_impl.cc`** - The unified/new gRPC mux implementation (template-based) supporting both SotW and Delta. Instantiated with both `DeltaSubscriptionState` and `SotwSubscriptionState`.

8. **`source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/new_grpc_mux_impl.cc`** - The new delta gRPC mux implementation.

9. **`source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/sotw_subscription_state.cc`** - SotW subscription state handling, creates `envoy::service::discovery::v3::DiscoveryRequest`.

10. **`source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/delta_subscription_state.cc`** - Delta subscription state handling.

11. **`source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/delta_subscription_state.cc`** - Legacy delta subscription state.

12. **`source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_subscription_factory.cc`** - Factory that creates gRPC subscriptions, selects between unified and legacy mux implementations.

13. **`source/common/config/protobuf_link_hacks.h`** - Forces linking of v3 service protos.

14. **`test/config/v2_link_hacks.h`** - Forces linking of v2 service protos (for testing).

15. **`source/server/proto_descriptors.cc`** - Validates that v3 proto descriptors are available.

16. **`source/common/config/api_version.h`** - Defines `API_NO_BOOST` macro for preventing API boosting.

The key aspects of the v2-to-v3 migration:
- `type_to_endpoint.cc` explicitly references both v2 and v3 service names in comments
- `utility.h` has `checkTransportVersion()` that rejects V2 transport
- The gRPC mux implementations use v3 proto types exclusively
- `v2_link_hacks.h` forces linking of v2 protos for backward compatibility in tests
- `protobuf_link_hacks.h` forces linking of v3 protos for production

The "dual-version subscription handling in the ADS gRPC server" is implemented in:
- `grpc_mux_impl.cc` (legacy SotW ADS)
- `xds_mux/grpc_mux_impl.cc` (unified SotW+Delta ADS)
- `new_grpc_mux_impl.cc` (new delta ADS)
- `grpc_subscription_factory.cc` (selects between unified and legacy based on runtime feature flag)

The versioned proto type conversion is handled in:
- `type_to_endpoint.cc` - maps type URLs to service methods
- `utility.h` - `checkTransportVersion()` validates API version

The v2-to-v3 field renames (e.g., DiscoveryRequest) are handled at the proto level in the API definitions, and the C++ code uses v3 types exclusively.

Let me also look for any specific files that handle the actual conversion between v2 and v3 proto types.

  "text": "The Envoy xDS v2-to-v3 API migration is implemented across several C++ source files in the sg-evals/envoy--v1.31.2 repository. Here is a detailed breakdown:\n\n## 1. Versioned Proto Type Conversion and Type URL Mapping\n\n**`source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.cc`** and **`source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.h`** are the central files for versioned proto type conversion. The file explicitly references both v2 and v3 service names in comments (e.g., `\"envoy.api.v2.RouteDiscoveryService\"` and `\"envoy.service.route.v3.RouteDiscoveryService\"`). The `buildTypeUrlToServiceMap()` function builds a reverse map from resource type URLs to v3 service methods (SotW gRPC, Delta gRPC, REST). The exported functions `sotwGrpcMethod()`, `deltaGrpcMethod()`, and `restMethod()` translate xDS resource type URLs to the corresponding v3 gRPC service method descriptors.\n\n## 2. V2-to-V3 Field Renames and Transport Version Validation\n\n**`source/common/config/utility.h`** contains the `checkTransportVersion()` template function (lines 155-169) which validates the `transport_api_version` field in `ApiConfigSource`. If the version is not `AUTO` or `V3`, it emits a deprecation warning: `\"V2 xDS transport protocol version is deprecated...The v2 xDS major version has been removed and is no longer supported.\"` This is the primary enforcement point for the v2-to-v3 migration.\n\n**`source/common/config/subscription_factory_impl.cc`** calls `Utility::checkTransportVersion(api_config_source)` (line 67) before creating any subscription, enforcing that v2 transport is rejected.\n\n## 3. Dual-Version Subscription Handling in the ADS gRPC Server\n\nThe dual-version (SotW vs Delta) subscription handling is implemented across multiple files:\n\n**`source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_mux_impl.cc`** — The legacy SotW ADS gRPC mux. Uses `envoy::service::discovery::v3::DiscoveryRequest/Response` exclusively. The `GrpcMuxFactory` registers as `\"envoy.config_mux.grpc_mux_factory\"` and creates `GrpcMuxImpl` for SotW ADS using `StreamAggregatedResources`.\n\n**`source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/grpc_mux_impl.cc`** — The unified/new gRPC mux (template-based). Instantiated as both `GrpcMuxImpl<DeltaSubscriptionState, ..., DeltaDiscoveryRequest, DeltaDiscoveryResponse>` and `GrpcMuxImpl<SotwSubscriptionState, ..., DiscoveryRequest, DiscoveryResponse>`. Registers `DeltaGrpcMuxFactory` (for `DeltaAggregatedResources`) and `SotwGrpcMuxFactory` (for `StreamAggregatedResources`).\n\n**`source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/new_grpc_mux_impl.cc`** — The new delta-only ADS gRPC mux using `DeltaDiscoveryRequest/Response`.\n\n**`source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_subscription_factory.cc`** — The factory that selects between unified and legacy mux implementations based on the `envoy.reloadable_features.unified_mux` runtime feature flag. `GrpcConfigSubscriptionFactory` creates SotW subscriptions (using `sotwGrpcMethod()`), `DeltaGrpcConfigSubscriptionFactory` creates delta subscriptions (using `deltaGrpcMethod()`), and `AdsConfigSubscriptionFactory` creates ADS subscriptions.\n\n**`source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/sotw_subscription_state.cc`** — Handles SotW subscription state, creates `envoy::service::discovery::v3::DiscoveryRequest` in `getNextRequestInternal()`, and processes `DiscoveryResponse` in `handleGoodResponse()`.\n\n**`source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/delta_subscription_state.cc`** — Handles delta subscription state for the unified mux.\n\n**`source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/delta_subscription_state.cc`** — Handles delta subscription state for the legacy mux, creates `DeltaDiscoveryRequest` in `getNextRequestAckless()` and `getNextRequestWithAck()`.\n\n## 4. Proto Linking and Descriptor Validation\n\n**`source/common/config/protobuf_link_hacks.h`** — Forces linking of all v3 service proto descriptors (ADS, SDS, LDS, RDS, CDS, EDS, SRDS, ECDS, RTDS, HDS) needed for the gRPC service method lookup.\n\n**`test/config/v2_link_hacks.h`** — Forces linking of v2 service proto descriptors (for backward compatibility in tests that still need to reference v2 types like `envoy::api::v2::LdsDummy`, `envoy::service::discovery::v2::AdsDummy`, etc.).\n\n**`source/server/proto_descriptors.cc`** — Validates at startup that all required v3 proto method and type descriptors are available in the generated pool.\n\n**`source/common/config/api_version.h`** — Defines the `API_NO_BOOST` macro used to prevent API boosting (automatic v2-to-v3 proto conversion) in specific files.\n\n## Summary of Migration Architecture\n\nThe v2-to-v3 migration in Envoy works as follows:\n1. All internal C++ code uses v3 proto types exclusively (`envoy::service::discovery::v3::DiscoveryRequest`, etc.)\n2. `checkTransportVersion()` in `utility.h` rejects any configuration that specifies V2 transport\n3. `type_to_endpoint.cc` maps resource type URLs to v3 service methods only\n4. The gRPC mux implementations (`grpc_mux_impl.cc`, `xds_mux/grpc_mux_impl.cc`, `new_grpc_mux_impl.cc`) all use v3 proto types\n5. The `grpc_subscription_factory.cc` selects between legacy and unified mux implementations\n6. Test files use `v2_link_hacks.h` to force-link v2 protos for backward compatibility testing"
}

## Key Files for xDS v2-to-v3 API Migration in Envoy

### 1. Versioned Proto Type Conversion
- **`source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.cc`** — Core file that maps xDS resource type URLs to versioned service methods. Comments explicitly reference both v2 (`envoy.api.v2.RouteDiscoveryService`) and v3 (`envoy.service.route.v3.RouteDiscoveryService`) service names. `buildTypeUrlToServiceMap()` builds the reverse map from type URLs to v3 service methods.
- **`source/common/config/type_to_endpoint.h`** — Exports `sotwGrpcMethod()`, `deltaGrpcMethod()`, `restMethod()`.

### 2. V2-to-V3 Field Renames / Transport Version Validation
- **`source/common/config/utility.h`** — `checkTransportVersion()` validates `transport_api_version` and rejects V2 with: *"V2 xDS transport protocol version is deprecated...The v2 xDS major version has been removed and is no longer supported."*
- **`source/common/config/subscription_factory_impl.cc`** — Calls `checkTransportVersion()` before creating any subscription.

### 3. Dual-Version Subscription Handling in ADS gRPC Server
- **`source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_mux_impl.cc`** — Legacy SotW ADS mux using `DiscoveryRequest/Response` v3 protos.
- **`source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/grpc_mux_impl.cc`** — Unified template-based mux instantiated for both SotW (`GrpcMuxSotw`) and Delta (`GrpcMuxDelta`), registering factories for both `StreamAggregatedResources` and `DeltaAggregatedResources`.
- **`source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/new_grpc_mux_impl.cc`** — New delta-only ADS mux.
- **`source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_subscription_factory.cc`** — Selects between unified/legacy mux based on runtime feature flag.
- **`source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/sotw_subscription_state.cc`** and **`delta_subscription_state.cc`** — Handle SotW/Delta subscription state and create v3 `DiscoveryRequest`/`DeltaDiscoveryRequest`.

### 4. Proto Linking
- **`source/common/config/protobuf_link_hacks.h`** — Forces linking of v3 service protos.
- **`test/config/v2_link_hacks.h`** — Forces linking of v2 service protos for backward compatibility in tests.
- **`source/server/proto_descriptors.cc`** — Validates v3 proto descriptors at startup.

### 3. Dual-Version (SotW + Delta) ADS gRPC Subscription Handling
- `source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/grpc_mux_impl.cc` — legacy SotW ADS mux using `DiscoveryRequest/Response` v3 protos

- `source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/sotw_subscription_state.cc` — builds `DiscoveryRequest` and processes `DiscoveryResponse`
- `source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/xds_mux/delta_subscription_state.cc` — delta state for unified mux
- `source/extensions/config_subscription/grpc/delta_subscription_state.cc` — delta state for legacy mux (`getNextRequestAckless`, `getNextRequestWithAck`)
